steps.title: Use the proximity finding
steps.content: Quito respondents reported higher park use than Bogota respondents, even though fewer Quito respondents lived within a 10-minute walk of a park. The text concludes that proximity alone cannot explain the difference in park use.

steps.title: Reject unsupported causes
steps.content: Choice A discusses researcher expectations, which the text never mentions. Choice B concerns when sources were calculated, not the cause of the difference. Choice D questions survey accuracy, but the text treats the survey results as evidence. The answer is C.
